description: Table "profiles" does not have Row Level Security (RLS) enabled. Any client with the anon key could potentially read/write all rows. RLS was disabled by migration_002 step 9.
fix:
Run: ALTER TABLE profiles ENABLE ROW LEVEL SECURITY;
Then add appropriate policies (see rls-policies.sql).

description: Foreign key column "activity.proposal_id" → proposals(id) has no index. JOINs, DELETEs, and UPDATEs on proposals will require sequential scans on activity.
fix: CREATE INDEX activity_proposal_id_idx ON activity (proposal_id);

description: "order" is a PostgreSQL reserved word (ORDER BY). It requires quoting.
fix: Rename column to "sort_order" or "display_order"

description: "role" is a PostgreSQL reserved word (CREATE ROLE). It requires quoting.
fix: Rename column to "contact_role" or "user_role"

description: "role" is a PostgreSQL reserved word (CREATE ROLE). It requires quoting.
fix: Rename column to "user_role" or "profile_role"

description: The "exec_sql" function is defined with SECURITY DEFINER, meaning it executes with the privileges of the function owner (typically a superuser). Although the current implementation only returns {"success": true} without executing the query, if someone modifies it to actually run SQL, it would be a critical security vulnerability — allowing any authenticated user to execute arbitrary SQL as a superuser.
fix:
Either:
1. Remove the function entirely if not needed: DROP FUNCTION exec_sql(text);
2. Change to SECURITY INVOKER to use caller's permissions
3. Add proper authorization checks inside the function

description: Column "google_calendar_connections.user_id" is TEXT type, but it references "profiles.id" which is UUID type. PostgreSQL may allow this with implicit casting, but it can cause performance issues and subtle bugs. The RLS policy also uses "auth.uid()::text = user_id" cast which prevents index usage on profiles.id.
fix:
ALTER TABLE google_calendar_connections ALTER COLUMN user_id TYPE uuid USING user_id::uuid;
Also update the RLS policy to use: auth.uid() = user_id (without ::text cast)

description: The "exec_sql" function is exposed as a public RPC endpoint (/rest/v1/rpc/exec_sql). Even though it currently doesn't execute queries, its existence could be exploited if the function is later modified. It also reveals internal architecture to potential attackers.

The "rls_auto_enable" function is exposed as a public RPC endpoint (/rest/v1/rpc/rls_auto_enable). This function likely enables RLS on tables, which is a DDL operation. If callable by anon users, it could be used to disrupt database security settings.
Table "tags" has no unique constraint on the "name" column. This allows duplicate tag names to be created (e.g., two "VIP" tags), which could cause confusion and data integrity issues.
fix: CREATE UNIQUE INDEX tags_name_unique_idx ON tags (name);

description: Table "deal_activities" has no unique constraint to prevent duplicate activity entries. Combined with no updated_at auto-update trigger, this could lead to duplicate records.
fix: Consider adding a unique constraint on (deal_id, type, title, created_at) or implement upsert logic in the application layer.

description: Table "workflow_rules" has no unique constraint on the "name" column. Multiple rules with the same name could cause confusion in the UI and automation logic.
fix: CREATE UNIQUE INDEX workflow_rules_name_unique_idx ON workflow_rules (name);
